title: Nezumi Steals Nami’s Hope
scene: On pages 9–12, Nojiko’s account of the past reveals how Nami, Bellemere, and Cocoyasi Village were damaged by Arlong’s arrival. The truth of Nami’s long sacrifice collides with Marine corruption and Arlong’s manipulation.

dialogue.speaker: Marine Captain Nezumi
dialogue.text: Search her house!

dialogue.speaker: Marine soldiers
dialogue.text: Yessir!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: W-Wait! You can't just enter other people's homes without permission!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I said stop!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Aghhh!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: What's the meaning of this...? Is this what Marines should be doing!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Don't you all have something more important to take care of!?

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: The ARLONG Pirates are going around killing people and destroying villages on this island! You know that, don't you!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: All the people on this island are slaves under their rule!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Yet you ignore that crisis and instead decide to confiscate the stolen goods of one petty thief!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Is that how the government prioritizes things!?

dialogue.speaker: Marine Captain Nezumi
dialogue.text: chichichi...! criminals shouldn't speak so high-handedly, little girl!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: Ignore her and continue on with the search!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Even though the people of this island have been waiting for years for the Marines to help...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: You pass them by without even so much as a glance and come right for me!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: captain, these orange fields seem rather suspicious...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Dig up the fields then!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Don't you dare touch Belliemere's oranges with your filthy hands!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Ugh!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: I'll never hand over my money to the likes of you!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: That money is...

dialogue.speaker: Cocoyasi villagers
dialogue.text: That money is for the sake of every resident here in Cocoyashi Village!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Do you still believe you have the right to take that money away!?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Hmm?

dialogue.speaker: Cocoyasi villagers
dialogue.text: Is that the way a mere village should be addressing a marine captain...?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Gen...?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: How did you... know that...?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: I knew all Arlong, naml...!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: back then, we couldn't believe what you had done no matter what,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: So we forced Nojiko to tell us the truth...

dialogue.speaker: Cocoyasi villagers
dialogue.text: Everyone in the village knew all Arlong.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: But we feared that if you knew that we knew,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: Our hopes would pressure you into staying if you ever wanted to run away from it all.

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: That's why we pretended not to know.

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: WHA...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: What on earth is the meaning of this?

dialogue.speaker: Cocoyasi villagers
dialogue.text: Does this mean that all the villagers in this town were abetting a thief so we should arrest all of you?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: They're saying that the government is hopeless,

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: So they have no choice but to fend for themselves!

dialogue.speaker: Nojiko
dialogue.text: Nojiko.

summary: This part deepens Nami’s backstory, showing how family, money, and survival became tied to Arlong’s control over the village.
